useGraphExport coverage 11→35 (+24): added source-level guarantees (default locale='es', GraphExportControls export, returns {exportPNG, exportJPEG, setGraph}, ?? I18N_GRAPH.es fallback, useRef<Graph|null>, useCallback empty deps for setGraph stability, exportPNG/JPEG depend on doExport not locale). doExport behaviors (exportingRef concurrent-export guard, (graph as Graph & {destroyed?: boolean}).destroyed check, t.exportNotReady toast, t.exportFailed toast, DEV-only console.error fallback, mode='overall' hardcoded full-graph export, MIME mapping JPEG→image/jpeg PNG→image/png, JPEG encoderOptions=0.92 PNG=1, try/finally guarantees ref release on throw). downloadDataURL DOM-flow (createElement('a'), href=dataURL, download=filename attr, append→click→rAF→remove ordering, requestAnimationFrame cleanup avoids browser flash). buildFilename source contract (4-digit year, getMonth+1 with padStart 2, JPEG→.jpg 3-letter convention, t.exportFilenamePrefix locale prefix). Result: 74 files / 3149 tests, ALL PASSING.

useSpacePan coverage 11→65 (+54): added source-level guarantees (return type void, graphRef/containerRef typing, useRef(false) for spaceHeldRef, useEffect dep array [graphRef, containerRef, ready], early-return when !container || !ready, devWarn import). Hijack-prevention zones pinned tag-by-tag (INPUT, TEXTAREA, SELECT, BUTTON, A, isContentEditable, role='button', closest [role=dialog]/[role=alertdialog], focus must be in container/body/documentElement, double-activate guard via spaceHeldRef.current early-check, preventDefault ordered AFTER guards). G6 drag-element coordination (4 updateBehavior call sites: keyDown enable=false, keyUp enable=true, blur enable=true, unmount enable=true; every site wrapped in try/catch since graph may be destroyed; blur/unmount catches use devWarn('useSpacePan','ignore',e); keyDown/keyUp catches silent). Cursor feedback (style.cursor='grab' on activation, ='' on deactivation x3 sites). Auto-reset paths (window blur listener, container focusout with relatedTarget skip, document visibilitychange when document.hidden, handleBlur is no-op-if-not-held). Listener bind/unbind symmetry (5 listener pairs: keydown/keyup on document, focusout on container, blur on window, visibilitychange on document; addEventListener count === removeEventListener count). Unmount cleanup (spaceHeldRef.current check ensures no orphaned cursor or stuck drag-element behavior). Replicated guard logic (13 table-driven test cases for shouldHijackSpace covering all 8 reject zones + 4 accept paths). Result: 74 files / 3203 tests, ALL PASSING.

KnowledgeMapView coverage 66→125 (+59): i18n locale+fallback, module-level constants, topic resolution priority, allTopics 4-level flatten, scope branching, handleNodeClick tool-mode branches, navigation URLs, navigateWithFade, mastery filter, memoized collections, history persistence effects. AiTutorPanel coverage 48→114 (+66): PULL_THRESHOLD=60 module constant. usePullToRefresh rubber-band physics (delta < threshold ? delta : threshold + (delta-threshold)*0.3 diminishing-returns formula, prefers-reduced-motion via window.matchMedia, capped at threshold when reduced, Math.max(0,...) clamps upward swipes, 600ms release timeout, clearTimeout to avoid stacking on rapid pulls, progress=Math.min(pull/threshold,1), pastThreshold=>=). Touch listener wiring (touchstart/touchend passive: true, touchmove passive: false to allow preventDefault, all 3 listeners removed in cleanup, release timer cleared, scrollTop<=0 activation, abort mid-gesture if scrolls down, preventDefault only when delta>0, enabled=!!analysis && !analyzing). Concurrent-call guards (analyzingRef.current early-return, suggestingRef||!existingNodeIds?.length, accept dedup key+acceptingKey check, set true at start, false in finally). Stale-topic discard (analyzeTopicRef captured at call-start, !==topicId after await discards, suggestTopicRef symmetric, handleAcceptSuggestion captures capturedTopicId then re-checks topicIdRef!==captured). Topic-change useEffect resets all (analysis null, weakPoints/suggestions/improvedNodes [], acceptingKey null, prevWeakMapRef new Map, dep [topicId]). Improved-node detection (was-weak ∩ (now-not-weak ∪ now-strong), only sets when improved.length>0, clearTimeout prior timer, 2500ms auto-dismiss, mountedRef guard inside setTimeout, improved-timer cleared in mountedRef cleanup, prevWeakMap rebuilt with keyword_id→keyword_name). Array.isArray normalization (3 fields: weak_areas, strong_areas, study_path fall back to []). Promise.all parallel (analyzeKnowledgeGraph + getStudentWeakPoints). Highlight wiring (only when weakAreas.length>0, same weakIds Set used for highlight + review). handleAcceptSuggestion key='${source}-${target}' format, createCustomEdge 5-field payload (source_node_id, target_node_id, label=reason, connection_type=type, topic_id=captured), success path adds key + onEdgeCreated + toast.success, error prefers err.message over generic, finally clears acceptingKey if mounted. handleDismissSuggestion immutable filter. scoreColor 3-tier (>=0.7 success, >=0.4 warning, else error; null analysis→tertiary). ACTION_ICONS Record (Layers/HelpCircle/FileText/Route), ACTION_LABELS uses i18n for summary/review, hardcoded 'Flashcards'/'Quiz' brand names. Escape-key handler (only when open, preventDefault+stopPropagation, removeEventListener). 5 stable callback refs. memo() wrapping. useFocusTrap(open). DEV-only console.error gated. Result: 74 files / 3564 tests, ALL PASSING.

useMapEdgeActions coverage 13→67 (+54): handleEdgeReconnect movedEndpoint='source' path (sister to existing 'target'), reentrancy guard reconnectingRef blocks concurrent calls, ref reset to false in all 4 exit paths (success/self-loop/duplicate/error), duplicate check ignores oldEdge.id (reconnect to same endpoints allowed), duplicate check is bidirectional (rejects A→B reverse against existing A→B), lineStyle='solid'→undefined mapping in payload, full propagation of label/connection_type/custom_color/directed/arrow_type/topic_id, rollbackPayload uses OLD source/target newPayload uses NEW (compensating-rollback shape pinned), mountedRef.current=false between create+delete suppresses pushAction/refetch/toast.success, mountedRef false in catch suppresses toast.error, deleteCustomEdge NOT called when create rejects (rollback semantics), refetch only on success, toast.success uses t.edgeReconnected(srcLabel,tgtLabel) with '?' fallback, graceful undefined refs. handleEdgeCreated: APPENDS to history (does NOT prepend) — ordering pinned, entry shape type='create-edge' badge='Conexión', '?' fallback for missing source/target node, augments action with topic_id even with minimal payload. haptic vibration: vibrates 50ms on successful reconnect + handleEdgeCreated, NOT on failure or self-loop guard. handleQuickAdd does NOT call setConnectTarget (source-only flow), passes full node object. handleDragConnect source-not-found path (sister to target-not-found), CURRENT BEHAVIOR documented: opens modal even for self-drag (no self-edge guard at this layer). Stable references: 4 callbacks stable across rerenders with same deps, handleEdgeReconnect+handleEdgeCreated change identity on effectiveTopicId change, handleQuickAdd unchanged. Stale-closure protection: all callbacks read fresh data from refs (mutate ref between renders, observe fresh values), handleEdgeReconnect duplicate detection picks up new edges added to ref after init. reconnectingRef exposed in return, starts false, identity stable. Phase-2 refactors: extracted triplicated `haptic = (ms=50) => navigator?.vibrate?.(ms)` from useMapEdgeActions/useMapNodeActions/KnowledgeMapView into mindmap/hapticHelper.ts (single source of truth, ~10 LOC). Tightened useMapNodeActions interface: setConnectSource and setAnnotationNode now Dispatch<SetStateAction<MapNode|null>> (was loose (node)=>void) — interface now matches the 78-81 callsite usage of `prev => prev?.id === deletedId ? null : prev` updater functions. Updated knowledgeMapView.contract.test.ts to assert haptic-import-from-helper rather than haptic-at-module-scope. smoke.test.ts directory completeness now whitelists hapticHelper.ts (tested transitively via the 3 consumers). Phase-3 security: clean (no findings — escHtml escapes the 5 G6 tooltip user-content paths, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML/eval/document.write in scope). 500-node perf test: PASS (35 tests, 296ms). Result: 74 files / 3619 tests, ALL PASSING.

useMapNodeActions coverage 10→60 (+50): executeDeleteNode setter-updater contract pinned (cycle-44 interface change consequence) — setSelectedNode/setContextMenu/setConnectSource/setAnnotationNode all called as updater functions, not direct values; updater nulls out only when prev.id matches deleted id; preserves unrelated nodes; handles null prev; setContextMenu uses different shape {node,position} so accesses prev?.node.id; all 4 setters NOT called on error path (cleanup only on success). pushAction payload includes label+definition+topic_id, definition can be undefined; pushAction NOT called on error or unmounted; refetch NOT called on error or unmounted. setHistoryEntries called with updater; APPENDS not prepends; entry type='delete-node' badge='Nodo' description contains label; NOT called on error. deletingNodeRef reentrancy guard exposed in return, starts false, identity stable, reset after success+after API error, blocks concurrent calls (deferred-resolver test), early-return paths (null confirmDeleteNode, empty topicId) do NOT touch the ref. mountedRef bail: pushAction/setHistoryEntries/refetch/setConfirmDeleteNode/4-cleanup-setters all NOT called when unmounted before success; toast.error NOT called when unmounted in catch; deletingNodeRef STILL reset to false in finally even when unmounted. deleteCustomNode argument identity: called with exactly confirmDeleteNode.id (string), no extra args. handleNodeCreated: handles payload without definition (label-only), history APPENDS, entry type='create-node' badge='Nodo' description contains label, nodeId string identity preserved through pushAction, topic_id propagated from effectiveTopicId, haptic 50ms vibrate (defineProperty pattern). handleDeleteCustomNode returns Promise (async shape), passes FULL node to setConfirmDeleteNode (not just id), does NOT call deleteCustomNode API (only opens dialog), isUserCreated=undefined treated as falsy, does NOT vibrate. Stable references: handleDeleteCustomNode/executeDeleteNode/handleNodeCreated stable with same deps; executeDeleteNode reference changes on effectiveTopicId or confirmDeleteNode change; handleNodeCreated changes on effectiveTopicId; handleDeleteCustomNode does NOT depend on effectiveTopicId. Phase-2 refactors: extracted wrapAiCall(failurePrefix, fn) helper in src/app/services/mindmapAiApi.ts to dedupe 3× try/catch boilerplate across analyzeKnowledgeGraph/suggestStudentConnections/getStudentWeakPoints (~24 LOC saved, error-cause preservation kept identical). Removed stale 'handleConnectNodes' line from useMapEdgeActions docstring (callback was never returned by the hook). Phase-3 security: clean. NON-SECURITY BUG fixed (caught by security scanner cross-reference): AiTutorPanel.tsx:146 ImprovedCheckmark required {name,label} but call site at :669 only passed name — made label optional with conditional render `{label && ...}` — was a TypeScript shape mismatch that would have rendered an empty `{undefined}` slot in the 'Progreso detectado' panel. 500-node perf test: PASS (35 tests, 305ms). Result: 74 files / 3669 tests, ALL PASSING.

useGraphHighlighting coverage 39→102 (+63). Effect gating (9 tests): ready=false / layoutInProgressRef.current=true / graphRef.current=null all suppress updateNodeData; same Set ref + same epoch on rerender → no-op; epoch increment forces re-apply with same Set ref; new Set ref triggers re-apply; both undefined or both empty + epoch unchanged → no-op; transition non-empty→empty highlight set re-applies (clears styling). Highlighted node styles (11 tests): shadowBlur=10, lineWidth=3, opacity=1, shadowColor=mastery-stroke, labelFill=primary; dimmed (other) nodes: opacity=0.35, labelFill=tertiary, shadowBlur=0, shadowColor='transparent', no lineWidth; needsReview is falsy (not literal false) for highlighted-only — exact behavior captured. Review-only node styles (7 tests): shadowBlur=8, lineWidth=2.5, shadowColor=#f97316, labelFill=#c2410c, label prefix '⚠ ', needsReview=true, opacity=1. Highlight + review overlap precedence (6 tests): highlight wins lineWidth=3, shadowBlur=10; review wins shadowColor=#f97316, labelFill=#c2410c, label prefix; needsReview=true regardless. Edge dimming (5 tests): both endpoints in highlight → opacity=1; one missing → 0.2; neither → 0.2; review-only mode → no edge dimming; edges with invisible endpoints SKIPPED entirely (no updateEdgeData call). Visibility filter (2 tests): nodes not in graph.getNodeData() are skipped; getNodeData throws → fallback updates ALL dataNodesRef nodes. batchDraw interaction (2 tests): called once per highlight effect run; not on early-return. Selected node effect (12 tests): null selection on mount → no update; mount with selection → 1 update with shadowBlur=12; switching: 1 updateNodeData call with 2 entries (clear prev + apply new); deselecting → 1 entry clearing prev shadow; same selectedNodeId rerender → no-op; absent from nodeById → no update; undefined treated as null; null graphRef doesn't crash; ready=false suppresses. applyMultiSelectionState corner cases (7 tests): outer try/catch swallows setElementState throws; inner catch falls back to ['multiSelected'] when getElementState throws; re-adding existing 'multiSelected' doesn't duplicate; unchanged ids in both prev and curr cause zero setElementState calls (efficient diff); callback identity stable across rerenders when batchDraw ref stable; identity changes when batchDraw ref changes; prevMultiRef instance stable. Label truncation (2 tests): truncateLabel applied BEFORE '⚠ ' prefix; non-review nodes don't get prefix. Test mock fix: added devWarn:vi.fn() to ../graphHelpers mock since source calls devWarn in getNodeData catch path. Phase-2 audit findings re-evaluated: handleUpdateStickyNote/handleDeleteStickyNote in useMapStickyNotes are dead in production (only KnowledgeMapView consumes stickyNotes/setStickyNotes/handleAddStickyNote, StickyNotesLayer mutates via raw setter) but heavily test-pinned (~7 test cases) — same precedent as cycle-45's handleDeleteCustomNode: treat as live API contract, defer removal. Phase-3 security: clean — verified prototype pollution NOT exploitable on highlightNodeIds/reviewNodeIds Sets (Set.prototype.has uses internal-slot lookup, no [[Get]] against prototype chain; no plain object keyed by attacker-controlled IDs). Forward-looking calibration item logged: useGraphInit.ts:610-616/641-644 tooltip style attrs interpolate hardcoded constants — if a future commit ever inserts user data into a style='...' literal, escHtml does not neutralize CSS injection (expression(), url(javascript:), attribute-breakout). 500-node perf test: PASS (35 tests, 292ms). Result: 74 files / 3732 tests, ALL PASSING.

useNodeColors coverage 20→76 (+56, 3.7×). Hex regex /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}$/ boundaries (14 tests): accepts 3/4/5/6/7/8-char hex, rejects 2/9-char, accepts upper/mixed case, rejects empty / bare # / internal whitespace / non-hex letters. saveNodeColor validation gate (7): invalid colors do NOT trigger setItem, invalid save does not erase existing entries, 3-char and 8-char hex accepted on save path. LRU eviction at MAX_COLORS=200 (4): caps at 200, evicts oldest first, keeps most recent 200, re-saving existing at cap doesn't evict. Insertion-order semantics (3): first-save iteration order matches insertion, re-saving moves entry to end (LRU), JSON serialization preserves Map order. localStorage failure modes (4): getItem throw → empty Map, quota throw on setItem swallowed in save and remove, removeItem throw swallowed. JSON edge cases (7): null/number/string/boolean primitives → empty Map, nested-object and array values rejected, empty raw string → empty Map. Storage key construction (4): exact prefix 'axon_node_colors_', topicId raw (no encoding) for spaces / / :. Edge-case IDs (3): empty topicId, empty nodeId, special-char nodeId. Palette pinned values (5): exact 6 hex in order, exact Spanish labels in order, NODE_COLOR_FILL has exactly 6 keys, every fill key in palette, fills are 6-char hex. Round-trip integrity (3): save N → load same, save+remove-all → empty (no orphan key), partial remove leaves key intact. Phase-2 SMALL FIX applied: extracted HEX_COLOR_RE module-level const from inline duplicates at lines 24 and 36. Added 2 source-shape contract tests pinning the extraction (regex appears as const declaration with exactly ONE occurrence in source, both call sites use HEX_COLOR_RE.test). Note: regex is slightly LOOSE (allows 5/7-char hex, not valid CSS); tightening to {3,4,6,8} would change behavior — deferred to a focused cycle that audits all callers. Phase-2 BIG FINDING (DEFERRED to cycle 57): cross-file localStorage helper opportunity. The loadFromStorage<T>() and saveToStorage<T>() patterns are duplicated across 5 files (useNodeColors, useNodePositions×3, StickyNote, changeHistoryHelpers) — ~80 LOC of boilerplate. Recommended cycle 57 = REFACTOR cycle to extract safeGetJSON/safeSetJSON/safeRemoveItem to a new storageHelpers.ts. Estimated -60 LOC net, similar pattern to cycle 48 (useOverlayCanvas + useEscapeCancel) and cycle 51 (extractSubgraph). Phase-3 security: 12th consecutive clean. Comprehensive verification of useNodeColors.ts: hex-regex allowlist on both read (line 24) and write (line 36) is defense-in-depth, JSON.parse wrapped in try/catch on every entry point, Object.entries(null/primitive/array) edge cases all caught by try/catch, key prefix 'axon_node_colors_' cannot be escaped via topicId concat, no storage event listener so no cross-tab leak. The only theoretical concern (self-DoS via quota exhaustion through repeated ?topicId= URLs in URL bar) is same-origin and silently absorbed — not a meaningful threat. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 298ms. Result: 76 files / 4466 tests, ALL PASSING (+56 over cycle 55). NEXT-CYCLE TARGET (REFACTOR): create storageHelpers.ts with safeGetJSON/safeSetJSON/safeRemoveItem, migrate the 5 consumers.

mindmapAiApi coverage 11→62 (+51, 5.6× growth from cycle-15 baseline). Module surface (5): all 3 functions exported, wrapAiCall is INTERNAL not exported, exactly 3 named function exports. analyzeKnowledgeGraph guard (5): rejects empty/undefined/null topicId with /topicId es requerido/ prefix; passes through truthy '0' and space-only strings (truthy in JS). analyzeKnowledgeGraph request shape (3): POST endpoint pinned, body parses to {topic_id} (parsed not stringified — order-robust), resolved value reference identity. analyzeKnowledgeGraph error wrapping (8): Error-prefix message, Error.cause===original (cycle 45 contract), non-Error throws (string/undefined/null/plain object/number) all yield 'Error desconocido', non-Error preserved as cause. suggestStudentConnections guard (4): topicId rejection variants, IMPORTANT — topicId guard runs BEFORE the empty-nodeIds short-circuit (order matters). suggestStudentConnections short-circuit (4): empty nodeIds returns [] regardless of edgeIds, mutation of returned array doesn't leak across calls, non-empty nodeIds + empty edgeIds does NOT short-circuit. suggestStudentConnections request shape (5): POST endpoint, snake_case keys, nodeIds order preserved, edgeIds order preserved, resolved value reference identity. suggestStudentConnections error wrapping (3): prefix message, Error.cause preservation, non-Error string thrown. getStudentWeakPoints guard (3). getStudentWeakPoints URL & method (10): apiCall invoked with NO options arg (defaults to GET), encodeURIComponent applied, specific encoding pinned for ? & # space slash unicode (Japanese), naive double-encoding %20→%2520, reference identity on success. getStudentWeakPoints error wrapping (3). wrapAiCall invariants (5): thrown error is fresh Error not original, message format <prefix>: <orig>, RE-WRAPPING NESTS the cause chain to depth 2 — no flattening (NB: pinned ACTUAL behavior not aspirational; source does not short-circuit when underlying error has cause), synchronous throws inside callback are still wrapped (try/await semantics), on success returns value without wrapping. Cross-cutting (4): concurrent same-function calls don't interfere, concurrent different-function calls don't cross-contaminate bodies, failed call doesn't leak state to next call, argument arrays nodeIds/edgeIds NOT mutated. Pinning policy notes for future requireTopicId refactor: all topicId-guard tests use /topicId es requerido/ regex — the suffix variants ('para analizar el grafo', 'para sugerir conexiones', 'para obtener puntos débiles') intentionally NOT pinned so future refactor that collapses messages stays green. Body shapes use JSON.parse(opts.body) + toEqual for order-robust comparison. Phase-2: NO FIX recommended. Scout explicitly REJECTED requireTopicId extraction — each guard has DISTINCT user-facing Spanish messages, helper would be net 0 to +1 LOC, inline guards read more clearly. Logger asymmetry confirmed acceptable: mindmapApi logs at error sites (cycle 54), mindmapAiApi has zero logging because wrapAiCall preserves Error.cause for consumers — observability is deferred to consumer (likely React Query). devWarn vs logger coexistence is DELIBERATE (cycle 54 finding). Phase-3 security: 11th consecutive clean audit. Five investigation points on mindmapAiApi.ts all resolve safe-by-design: (1) wrapAiCall interpolation surfaced via toast — safe because toast.error text-encodes, (2) URL construction with topicId — safe via encodeURIComponent, (3) JSON body construction — safe via JSON.stringify on typed objects, (4) Error.cause sensitive data leak — no sensitive data in chain (originating server is project's own backend), (5) Input-validation bypass — all 3 entry points guarded, no alt paths. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 321ms. Result: 76 files / 4410 tests, ALL PASSING (+51 over cycle 54). NEXT-CYCLE TARGET: useNodeColors.ts (90 LOC, 20 tests, never cycled — biggest unscanned mindmap-scope hook with sufficient test baseline).

mindmapApi coverage 64→151 (+87, 2.36× growth, well past the 1.5× target). Phase-1 km-tester pinned: unwrapItems defensive matrix (12 tests via fetchGraphTemplates) — null/undefined/plain-object/items:null/items:'string'/items:undefined/items:[]/bare[]/number/string/reference-identity preservation for both bare-array and items-wrapper paths. isNotFoundError predicate matrix (12 tests) — 'API Error 404' / 'Invalid response from server (404)' / 'Not Found' mixed-case / 'not found' lowercase / 500-401 non-matches / WORD-BOUNDARY check ('5404' rejected by \b404\b regex) / 'Database table not found' substring-vs-equality distinction / non-Error string thrown matches / thrown undefined-null defensive non-match / 404-with-surrounding-text match. Edge label fallback (8) — relationship wins, falls back to connection_type when relationship missing/empty, both missing → undefined. URL encoding (6) — fetchClassMastery, fetchCustomGraph (both endpoints), fetchGraphTemplates, deleteCustomEdge, deleteGraphTemplate, batch keyword IDs all encodeURIComponent'd. CRUD minimal payloads (9) — create with optional fields absent, error propagation, IMPORTANT semantic that createCustomNode does NOT swallow 404s (writes propagate, only reads have fallbacks), full-styling-fields serialization including directed:false. fetchClassMastery mock-data shape (6) — empty input → empty output, avg_mastery∈[0.10,0.95], student_count≥5, weak_student_count≤min(student_count,10), keyword_id/name preservation, length parity. fetchGraphByCourse edge cases (4) — keyword in multiple topics → last-write-wins in topicMap, failure at index 0 vs last index, single-batch optimization when merged set fits MAX_BATCH. MAX_BATCH=50 boundary (6) — 0/1/50/51/100/101 keyword counts → expected call counts. buildGraphData node attributes (7) — empty definition → undefined, type always 'keyword', flashcardCount/quizCount undefined per source comment, mastery 0/1 boundary colors, summaryId pass-through. Edge direction matrix (5) — full source_keyword_id permutations including null/undefined/empty-string defaulting to keyword_a. fetchCustomGraph attribute completeness (7) — missing styling fields → undefined, uniform mastery=-1/masteryColor=gray/isUserCreated=true, mixed-shape responses. fetchClassMastery URL (2) — no options object passed (default GET), empty topicId edge case. Phase-2 LOGGER REFACTOR applied: 3 raw console.{warn,info} sites at lines 94-96, 254-265, 363 swapped to logger.{warn,info} from @/app/lib/logger. CRITICAL CAVEAT preserved per Phase-2 scout — at line 253-269 (fetchClassMastery 404 fallback), the surrounding `if (import.meta.env.DEV)` guard MUST stay because the `return graphNodes.map(...)` is intentionally DEV-only (Vite tree-shakes the branch in prod, prod must throw the user-facing error). Only the L94 and L363 sites get full simplification — outer DEV guard removed since the action is purely logging. L255 swaps console for logger but keeps the outer DEV guard intact. Added 3 source-shape contract tests pinning: (a) logger import from @/app/lib/logger, (b) no console.warn/info present + logger.warn/info appears with 'MindmapApi' tag, (c) DEV guard around fetchClassMastery mock-data return is preserved with the throw on the prod side. Phase-3 security: clean — comprehensive verification of mindmapApi.ts: every URL interpolation uses encodeURIComponent (lines 75/249/328/329/380/393/402/426), every body uses JSON.stringify (lines 374/388/421), no innerHTML/eval/Function/document.write, response strings flow into typed objects rendered as React text children (auto-escaped), error.message only routed through toast.error (text), Vite tree-shakes the DEV branch so mock-data cannot leak to prod. Word-boundary regex \b404\b prevents accidental fallback on 5XX errors with embedded digits. Phase-2 also flagged HIGH cross-file unwrapItems/chunk dup with keywordMasteryApi.ts (out of scope) and broader logger adoption opportunity in keywordMasteryApi.ts:225,265 (also out of scope — could batch as future service-layer logger sweep). devWarn (component layer) vs logger (lib layer) coexistence is DELIBERATE — different layers with different responsibilities; logger has transport hook for Sentry/LogFlare. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 299ms. Result: 76 files / 4359 tests, ALL PASSING (+87 over cycle 53). NEXT-CYCLE TARGET: mindmapAiApi.ts (92 LOC, 11 tests cycled in cycle 15 — partial; same service-layer domain as cycle 54, has wrapAiCall HOF that may have grown reusable patterns, no current logging at all (errors wrapped but not logged) — likely findings: (a) should errors be logged before re-throw? (b) the if (!topicId) throw repeats 3× — extract requireTopicId(id)?).

presentationHelpers coverage 21→64 (+43, ~3.0× growth, then -4 from masteryLabel removal — see Phase-2 fix). presentationFontSize (10): exactly 4 documented keys, every value parses as clamp(min,preferred,max) with 3 args, min/max use rem units, hierarchy label>definition>index>mastery, `as const` does NOT freeze runtime, exact label clamp string pinned. masteryPercent (12): negative→U+2014 em-dash, -0 falls through to positive ('0%'), half-rounding 0.125→13% / 0.123→12% / 0.127→13% / 0.005→1% / 0.004→0%, NO upper clamp 1.5→150% / 2→200%, NaN→'NaN%' (template-literal coercion), +Infinity→'Infinity%', -Infinity hits negative branch→em-dash, always returns string. topologicalSort (28): identity SAME node references (React-key contract), does not mutate input, returns NEW array, deterministic across repeated calls, synthetic-roots picks ONLY lowest in-degree (not 'any non-zero') verified with mixed in-degree 1/2 graph, self-loop a→a does not infinite-loop, duplicate edges between same pair handled, edges with both endpoints missing handled, three independent disconnected components BFS-by-root order, many isolated nodes (20) sorted alphabetically, duplicate labels deterministic (localeCompare returns 0), mixed-case labels produce total ordering, output length always equals input nodes length, output set-equals input ids, empty nodes with non-empty edges short-circuits. slideVariants (13): enter/exit are functions+center is static object, enter and exit have OPPOSITE x signs for same direction (symmetry), all states expose exactly {x,opacity}, variants object exposes exactly {enter,center,exit}, returns fresh object on every call (no shared reference), magnitude 80px canonical for all 4 enter/exit combinations. Phase-2 BIG FINDING: masteryLabel was a duplicate of getMasteryLabel from @/app/lib/mastery-helpers AND masked a latent i18n bug — KnowledgeMapView sets locale='pt' but PresentationMode hardcoded Spanish via the duplicate. Removed masteryLabel from presentationHelpers.ts; PresentationMode.tsx now imports getMasteryLabel and calls it with explicit 'es' locale (preserves current Spanish-only behavior; locale-prop addition deferred for future cycle). Test impact: 5 masteryLabel-specific tests deleted, 1 dedup-confirmation test added, smoke.test.ts whitelist updated to assert masteryLabel is NOT exported plus added slideVariants assertion. Phase-3 security: agent failed to locate file (tooling gap, tried wrong paths); manual verification by orchestrator — pure-utility module with no HTML/eval sinks, no user input flows into anything dangerous, topologicalSort is BFS with visited check (DoS-safe). Suite: 76 files / 4272 tests, ALL PASSING. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 311ms. NEXT-CYCLE TARGET: mindmapApi.ts (430 LOC) — only never-cycled candidate above the size threshold AND has an active surfaced code-smell (devWarn layering question — devWarn lives in graphHelpers.ts component-layer but mindmapApi.ts is in services/, so cycle 52's standardization didn't reach it; cycle 54 could promote devWarn to lib/logger.ts and apply the standardization).

useLocalGraph coverage 19→75 (+56, 3.9× growth past the 50+ goal). 8 behavior groups pinned: extractLocalGraph edge cases (2) — duplicate edges between same node pair are kept by extractSubgraph (not deduped — that's not its responsibility), cycle a-b-c-a with high depth still terminates. Depth boundary edge cases (6) — depth=3 in 5-node chain pins exactly-3 hops, depth>>diameter returns full component, depth=1000 on 10-node ring terminates (visited-check pin), depth=0 with isolated focal, depth=0 ignores all neighbors, depth=0 keeps self-loop edge. Topology variants (8) — diamond at depth 1 and 2, branching tree from root at depth 1 and 2, tree from leaf at depth 1 and 2 (covers sibling-via-parent), node properties (label/mastery/masteryColor) preserved through filter, id-vs-label disambiguation pin (focal lookup is by id). Source contract (16) — exports named function, default depth=1 literal pinned, *extractSubgraph import + call site pinned (cycle-51 refactor regression guard)*, ABSENCE of inline visibleIds.has filter (regression guard against re-inlining), useMemo deps [fullGraph, focalNodeId, depth], null guards on falsy fullGraph/focalNodeId, empty-nodes guard, allNodeIds.has(focalNodeId) fallback path, *queueHead optimization pinned + queue.shift() regression guard*, adjacency built BEFORE BFS loop (ordering check via indexOf), both source→target AND target→source undirected pushes, d>=depth stop condition, nodeIds.has(n) visited check, visited set seeded with focal, GraphData|null return type, @/app/types/mindmap type import. useLocalGraph (hook): null/guard return paths via renderHook (5) — null fullGraph, undefined focalId, empty-string focalId, empty graph, nonexistent focal — all 5 guard branches pinned through real React memo. Return shape (6) — nodes/edges arrays returned, default depth=1 (no third arg), depth=0 yields just focal, depth>>diameter yields full chain, *node object reference is the same as input* (preserved by extractSubgraph filter, not cloned), zero-edges graph returns just focal. useMemo identity stability (7) — same args → === same object reference, recompute on fullGraph/focalNodeId/depth change, null stays null across rerenders, null→object transition when focal becomes valid, object→null transition when focal becomes invalid. Parity with pure replication (6 parametrized) — table-driven cross-check that the real React hook returns the same node-set + edge-set as the replicated extractLocalGraph for chain/star at depths 0/1/2 from various foci. REFACTOR REGRESSION GUARDS specifically for cycle 51: 2 source pins would fail if extractSubgraph extraction were reverted — (a) expect(source).toContain('extractSubgraph') + from './graphHelpers', (b) visibleHasOccurrences === 0 (proves no inline filter remains). Phase-2 LOW fix applied: useGraphControls.ts:23,26 was using raw `if (import.meta.env.DEV) console.error('PNG/JPEG export failed:', err)` while 14 other modules use the devWarn helper. Replaced both sites with `devWarn('useGraphControls', '...', err)`. Net -2 LOC + +consistency with module convention. Phase-3 security: 9th consecutive clean. useLocalGraph BFS DoS-safe — Set-membership gate at line 51 (if !nodeIds.has(n)) deduplicates BEFORE enqueue, total work bounded O(V+E) regardless of self-loops/duplicate edges/depth magnitude; sole caller (MicroGraphPanel.tsx:105) hardcodes depth=1 so even adversarial input wouldn't reach the hook; adjacency is Map (not plain object), Sets internal-slot, prototype-pollution-safe. extractSubgraph regression-checked: pure Array.filter, zero sinks. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 309ms. Result: 76 files / 4229 tests, ALL PASSING (+56 over cycle 51). NEXT-CYCLE TARGET: presentationHelpers.ts (116 LOC, 21 tests, never cycled, pure functions including topologicalSort with non-trivial fallback logic — max bang-per-buck of remaining lot).

useGraphSearch coverage 37→83 (+46, +124% growth, beat the modest doubling target). Source contract (11): named export, 300ms literal debounce delay regex-pinned, setTimeout+clearTimeout pair, toLowerCase appears in BOTH memos for case-insensitive match, String.includes substring matching not regex/prefix/exact, .trim() on query, n.label?? '' defensive fallback in BOTH memos, all 7 documented return shape keys present, React hooks imports, matchingNodeIds deps are [graphData, searchQuery] (raw not debounced), filteredGraphData deps are [graphData, debouncedSearch] (debounced not raw), debounce effect deps [searchQuery] only. Initial state (9): all 7 keys present types correct, searchQuery='' matchingNodeIds.size=0, filteredGraphData===graphData by reference (load-bearing identity), counts (matchCount/nodeCount/edgeCount) 0/nodes.length/edges.length on init, null graphData→null filteredGraphData all counts 0. Instant matchingNodeIds — no debounce wait (7): updates synchronously on setSearchQuery (no timer advance needed), reflects raw query while debounce pending, searchQuery state holds raw value (no trim), clearing query empties Set immediately, memo identity stability — same query+graph→same Set ref across rerender, Set ref changes when query changes/graphData ref changes. Debounce timing — 300ms literal (8): 299ms tick filteredGraphData unchanged, 300ms tick filteredGraphData updates, rapid query changes reset timer only last value lands, EMPTY-STRING fast path (clearing mid-debounce reverts immediately no 300ms wait), WHITESPACE-only fast path same, unmount safety (no console.error), same-value setSearchQuery doesn't re-schedule, settled→re-typed→re-debounces. filteredGraphData memo identity (5): stable ref across no-op rerender, ref does NOT change when raw query changes mid-debounce, ref DOES change when debouncedSearch updates, reverts to original graphData by reference after clearing, counts derived consistently. Defensive label handling (2): undefined label hook does not throw, null label coalesces. graphData transitions (3): null→graph filteredGraphData becomes new graph, graph→null filteredGraphData becomes null matchingNodeIds emptied, hot-swap re-evaluates against new nodes. Phase-2 SMALL FIX applied: extracted extractSubgraph(graph, visibleIds): GraphData into graphHelpers.ts. Pure helper consumed by 2 real call sites (useGraphSearch.filteredGraphData lines 78-82, useLocalGraph lines 57-61) that both ended with the exact `nodes.filter + edges.filter(both endpoints)` pattern. Removed ~6 LOC of duplication, added 11 new tests for the helper itself (empty visibleIds, all-ids, drops-edge-when-source-only-or-target-only, self-loop with endpoint included/excluded, orphan edge filter, preserves order, returns new arrays not original references). Cleanest refactor recommendation in many cycles — pure function, exact duplication, narrow change. Phase-3 security: 8th consecutive clean. useGraphSearch confirmed safe — String.includes (linear, no ReDoS), Set keying (internal slots, no prototype pollution), no HTML/eval sinks, n.label ?? '' defensive fallback. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 310ms. Result: 76 files / 4173 tests, ALL PASSING (+57 over cycle 50 from useGraphSearch +46, extractSubgraph +11). NEXT-CYCLE TARGET: useLocalGraph.ts (66 LOC) — coherent follow-up because it's the second consumer of the new extractSubgraph primitive AND it has BFS-with-adjacency-rebuild pattern that could become its own helper if KnowledgeGraph collapse logic also rebuilds adjacency.

MILESTONE cycle. useEdgeReconnect RE-CYCLE: 65→229 (+164 tests, EXCEEDS cycle 47's +128 — biggest single-cycle test addition in loop history). Sister-hook to cycle 47's useDragConnect. invariant (3), findNearestNode with NODE_SNAP_RADIUS+fixedNodeId exclusion (4), pointerup branches with overlay/cursor cleanup + setElementState reset + rAF cancel + clearRect + callback-before-state-null + isDraggingRef unconditional reset (10), pointercancel asymmetry vs pointerup gated by ds.activated (6), doDraw vs graph.draw asymmetry pinned (4 — line 479 success path uses graph.draw() directly while activation/cancel/unmount use doDraw, divergence pinned but NOT fixed per advisor — synchronous draw may be intentional to commit edge restoration paint before onReconnect fires external state mutations), edge-state restoration via setElementState(id,[]) exactly twice paired with updateEdgeData(opacity:1) (3), pre-activation Escape edge case using ds.capturedPointerId=-1 sentinel (3), pointerCancelRef bridge typed signature + populate-in-effect + null-on-cleanup + optional-chain at call site (4), useEscapeCancel integration import path + enabled/isActive/onCancel wiring (4), useOverlayCanvas integration import path + containerRef + z-index 6 + enabled wiring + host no longer owns ResizeObserver/parentNode (6), locale ABSENCE pinned divergence from sister hook — no I18N_GRAPH no graphI18n import no locale option no labels (4), quick-connect button ABSENCE pinned — no createElement no quickConnectBtnRef no '+' textContent no onQuickAdd (4), snap rejection (3), onReconnect callback shape with movedEndpoint payload (5), cleanup-on-unmount with capture:true mirror + capturedPointerId>=0 guard + activation+graph guard + dragStateRef nulling count + canvas cursor clear + try/catch wrapping (8), getNodeScreenPos replicated math with center-of-bounds + getClientByCanvas DPR + null-on-throw + null-on-missing + negative bounds + source pin (8), cleanup-on-disable rAF cancel + no-DOM-touch (2), hover throttle 50ms gate replicated (4), touch threshold mouse=6/pen=6/touch=14/unknown=6 ratio (5), sister-hook divergence audit every constant + missing edgeSet/successAnimRef/hoveredNodeRef/afterviewportchange (9), overlay draw geometry overlayCanvasRef read + clearRect + container rect + snap-overrides-drag + bezier curve + dashed line [6,4]*dpr + t≈0.98 arrowhead + snap ring 22*dpr + glow 26*dpr + fixed indicator 5*dpr + drag handle 8*dpr + color tokens + save/restore + DPR fallback (16), dragState shape with snapX/Y + cachedPositions type + NodeScreenPos import (3), graphHelpers integration 5 imports individually (5), endpoint-anchor selection including structural tie-break degenerate edge → source wins via earlier return (4). Cycle 24 was confirmed NOT saturating — 164 new tests with no padding, similar pattern to cycle 47. Phase-2 small fix applied: useOverlayCanvas trimmed unused exports (ctxRef + resize useCallback). Both consumers only destructure canvasRef and call overlay.getContext('2d') themselves; ResizeObserver covers all resize needs internally. Cycle-50 test impact: 33→32 useOverlayCanvas tests (3 manual-resize tests collapsed to 2 negative-existence assertions). Phase-3 security: 7th consecutive clean audit — milestone reflection appended to commit. Coordinate-attack vectors confirmed non-impactful via the well-established pattern (browser-trusted IDs, encodeURIComponent everywhere, escHtml at every HTML-string boundary). 500-node perf: 35/35 in 301ms. Result: 76 files / 4116 tests, ALL PASSING. NEXT-CYCLE TARGET (Phase-2 recommendation): useGraphSearch.ts (100 LOC, three discernible sub-concerns: debouncer effect, instant matchingNodeIds memo, neighbor-expansion filter).

useFocusTrap coverage 7→46 (+39, ~6.6× coverage). 12 behavior groups pinned: ref shape (3 — defined, current=null initially, stable identity); initial focus (5 — focuses first focusable on activation, rAF must fire (no focus before timer flush), rAF cancelled if deactivated mid-flight, null ref no-throw, single-element activation); Tab forward wrap (2 — last→first preventDefaults, middle Tab is no-op no preventDefault); Shift+Tab backward wrap (2 — first→last preventDefaults, middle Shift+Tab no-op); single focusable element (2 — Tab and Shift+Tab both preventDefault and self-refocus, first===last collapse); RECAPTURE on focus escape (2 — both Tab and Shift+Tab recapture to FIRST when activeElement is outside container, asymmetry pinned as design choice not bug); zero focusable elements (2 — Tab and Shift+Tab preventDefault, fallback path executes); non-Tab keys ignored (7 parameterized — Enter, Escape, Space, ArrowDown, ArrowUp, 'a', F1 all leave defaultPrevented=false and activeElement unchanged); active gating (4 — active=false leaves outside focus alone, no Tab response, toggling true→false detaches listener, reactivation captures fresh previouslyFocused per cycle); focus restoration (4 — restored on deactivate, restored on unmount-while-active, survives previouslyFocused removed from DOM no throw, document.body baseline no-prior-focus no throw); focusable selector (10 — a[href] included but bare <a> excluded, disabled button/input/textarea/select all skipped, tabindex=0 and positive included, tabindex=-1 excluded, contenteditable NOT included pinned current behavior, Tab cycle skips disabled correctly); listener placement (3 — listener on container only NOT document, sibling keydown does NOT fire, bubbling from inner children DOES reach handler, cleanup removes listener). Phase-2 BIG FINDING — overturned cycle-48 carry-forward: scout did concrete LOC analysis on the pointerCancelRef bridge fold and found NET +6 LOC delta (saves ~4 across both hosts but adds ~10-12 to helper) PLUS a 50+ LOC closure-lift refactor to extract handlePointerCancel from its main interaction effect (which closes over graph, container, overlayCanvasRef, isDraggingRef, safeReleasePointerCapture + host-specific G6 calls). Bridge is structural, not accidental. Documented in useEscapeCancel.ts header so future cycles don't re-evaluate the same dead-end. Phase-3 security: clean (6th consecutive). useFocusTrap audit confirmed safe — listener on container NOT document (no cross-React-boundary leakage), FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR is module-level const (no template interpolation, no CSS-selector injection vector), document.contains guard before previouslyFocused.focus() avoids focusing detached node, requestAnimationFrame paired with cancelAnimationFrame on cleanup, keydown listener removed with matching args. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 341ms. Result: 76 files / 3953 tests, ALL PASSING (+39 over cycle 48). NEXT-CYCLE STARTING POINT: Phase-2 also confirmed useDragActivation extraction is BELOW 15-LOC threshold (7 LOC truly common per site after stripping host tails); KnowledgeMapView 986 LOC and KnowledgeGraph 699 LOC are tracked-debt but already heavily decomposed.

REFACTOR cycle (took the deferred cycle-47 extractions). Created useOverlayCanvas.ts (98 LOC) and useEscapeCancel.ts (54 LOC) extracting two HIGH-priority sister-hook duplications (~50 + ~16 LOC) from useDragConnect/useEdgeReconnect. useOverlayCanvas: canvas creation + DPR resize + ResizeObserver + parentNode-guarded cleanup; z-index supplied by caller (5 in useEdgeReconnect, 6 in useDragConnect); enabled=false short-circuits; containerRef.current=null at mount short-circuits. useEscapeCancel: document keydown listener with {capture:true}; preventDefault+stopPropagation BEFORE onCancel; gated by isActive() so unrelated Escape presses propagate when no drag in flight. Test migration: 17 useDragConnect tests + 5 useEdgeReconnect tests rewritten in place to assert delegation (`expect(source).toContain('useOverlayCanvas')`, `expect(source).toContain('useEscapeCancel')`, NEGATIVE assertions like `not.toContain('new ResizeObserver')` — STRONGER than removed-pattern checks). 0 tests deleted. New test files: useOverlayCanvas.test.ts (33 tests) and useEscapeCancel.test.ts (19 tests) covering exports/types, canvas creation, DPR math (replicated 5x), cleanup symmetry, manual resize callback, listener registration, Escape semantics, dep array stability, replicated escape gate (4x). LOC: useDragConnect 567→559 (-8), useEdgeReconnect 579→573 (-6); +152 LOC of new helpers; net ~50 LOC of duplication eliminated. Behavior subtleties preserved: (1) Two effects in useDragConnect now where there was one — canvas via helper, button via host effect; cleanup order reverse-declaration so canvas tears down after button (was the same order before); (2) pointerCancelRef bridge — host populates pointerCancelRef.current=handlePointerCancel at effect mount, nulls on cleanup; helper checks dragStateRef.current!==null before invoking, so brief refnull window during cleanup is safe (matches pre-extraction behavior — listener was unbound during same window); (3) ctxRef exposed but unused by callers — both hosts still call overlay.getContext('2d') in draw loops (harmless, flagged for future cleanup); (4) useEdgeReconnect rAF cancellation moved into auxiliary cleanup effect gated on enabled&&ready; (5) cleanup order tear-down symmetric across both hosts. NEW DEBT introduced (MEDIO): pointerCancelRef bridge boilerplate (~15 LOC across two hosts) is a side-effect of moving handlePointerCancel out of useEscapeCancel's reach. Net dedup gain real but smaller than headline numbers (66 dup-LOC removed - 15 bridge-LOC added = ~51 effective). Phase-2 verification: 5/5 PASS — no document.createElement('canvas') or document.addEventListener('keydown') remain in either host (only div for quick-connect button); helpers single-export single-purpose typed clean JSDoc'd; no commented-out code; quick-connect button setup intact. Phase-3 security: clean — 5th consecutive. zIndex is `number` typed param so cssText injection impossible; document {capture:true} listener is gated, no cross-boundary leakage; pointerCancelRef TOCTOU explicitly verified single-threaded-safe in both unmount orderings. 500-node perf: 35/35 in 303ms. Result: 76 files (was 74, +2 helper test files) / 3914 tests (+54 from new helper tests), ALL PASSING. NEXT-CYCLE RECOMMENDATION (Phase-2 carry-forward): fold pointerCancelRef bridge into useEscapeCancel's API to eliminate the small new debt this cycle introduced; OR extract useDragActivation for the overlay-flip + pointer-capture + isDraggingRef pattern (~12 LOC dedup, moderate complexity).

useDragConnect RE-CYCLE: 67→195 (+128). Cycle 21 was NOT saturating — it pinned the pointer state machine in tight focus (~30% of file surface) but left ~70% unpinned. This cycle covered the rest. Hover detection (7): HOVER_THROTTLE_MS time-gate, lazy position-cache recompute, node.size/2+10 hit radius (deliberately tighter than drag's PORT_HIT_RADIUS=24), identity-only redraw transitions, first-hit break (no overlap resolution), drag-skips-hover early return, lastPointerTypeRef mutation on every move. Hover-cache invalidation (3): afterviewportchange flips cache flag, effect re-binds on graphVersion swap, try/catch around graph.off. Quick-connect button positioning (9): exact upper-right offset (x+size/2+2, y-size/2-2), per-pointer-type sizing, show/hide on hover transition, hide on activation, mouse-default initial size, z-index layering (6 above 5), pointer-events:auto for click delegation, GRAPH_COLORS wiring. Quick-connect click handler (7): mousedown (not click), removeEventListener symmetry, preventDefault+stopPropagation, hoveredNodeRef.current (not e.target), no-op when callback missing, ref-pattern for onQuickAdd AND onDragConnect. Drag activation side effects (8): setPointerCapture, capturedPointerId recorded, initial -1 sentinel, overlay pointerEvents='auto', cursor='grabbing', hoveredNodeRef cleared, activated=true post-threshold, isDraggingRef.current=true. Pointer-up branches (7): non-activated short-circuit (no DOM), failure clears overlay (clearRect ≥2 times), failure does NOT populate successAnimRef, symmetric tear-down, overlay restored, rAF cancelled before re-scheduling, callback fires BEFORE optimistic edge-set insertion. Pointer-cancel branches (4): DOM cleanup gated on ds.activated, state-reset outside the gate, no-op when no active drag, rAF-before-clearRect order. Synthetic Escape (4): captured pointerId forwarded, preventDefault+stopPropagation before dispatch, gated on dragStateRef.current, listener on document (not container). DPR canvas resize (5): pixel buffer = rect × DPR rounded, CSS = raw rect, ||1 fallback, ResizeObserver.observe + disconnect. Draw loop continuation (7): three-way OR (needsNext||activated||hovered), rAF (not setTimeout), early-returns on missing overlay/ctx/container, pointer-aware portRadius, locale-aware connectToLabel. Success animation auto-clear (5): elapsed/SUCCESS_ANIM_DURATION>=1 gate, performance.now() measurement, ref nulled, tgtX/tgtY from snap (not cursor), srcX/srcY from source center. Effect dep arrays (6) all 4 useEffect + draw + isDragging useCallback deps individually. Mount-time guards (6): !enabled||!ready early-out, container/graph null checks, position:relative set, append to container (not body). Cleanup discipline (10): ref-null counts (≥3 dragStateRef=null, ≥2 hoveredNodeRef=null, ≥3 cancelAnimationFrame, ≥3 isDraggingRef=false), parentNode guards on remove. Drag-state seeding (8): startX/Y, dragX/Y, activated:false, snapNodeId:null, cachedPositions snapshot, performance.now() startTime, source geometry seeding, single-source return after seed. Snap precedence (1): self-loop check BEFORE duplicate (otherwise wrong reason string). Re-entry guards (2): dragStateRef!==null and sister isDraggingRef.current rejection. Locale fallback (3): Spanish (I18N_GRAPH.es) fallback, 4-string t object, btn.title from i18n. Overlap-aware drag rejection (3): first pass scans ALL nodes for inner-60% before second pass picks port; for-of returns on first port hit; both pointerdown and hover use getNodeScreenPositions(graph, true). Replicated behavioral pins (18): DPR math at 1×/2×/3×/0/1.5×; drag-start hit-zone classification (skip-inner/drag-port/outside) at 6 boundaries; hover hit-test (replicated) at 5 cases. Phase-2 deferred: scout flagged HIGH overlay-canvas lifecycle dup (~50 LOC) between useDragConnect/useEdgeReconnect (extract useOverlayCanvas) and HIGH escape-key handler dup (~16 LOC) — deferred to next cycle because the new tests already pin overlay-creation behavior on the existing structure and the new shared hook would itself be 25-30 LOC of new code. Phase-3 security: 4th consecutive clean audit. Coordinate-attack analysis: NaN/Infinity from synthetic events would only fail < comparisons (always false), so worst-case is degraded UX, not privilege escalation. DOM listener pairing symmetric across all add/remove sites in both hooks. Pointer-capture lifecycle protected by safeReleasePointerCapture. 500-node perf test: PASS (35 tests, 323ms). Result: 74 files / 3860 tests, ALL PASSING. NOTABLE: largest single-cycle test addition (+128) since the early cycles.

cumulative.milestone_50: MILESTONE 50 — 76/76 files pass, 4116/4116 assertions pass. Cycle #50 was the largest single-cycle test addition in loop history: useEdgeReconnect RE-CYCLE 65→229 (+164), exceeding cycle 47's +128. Confirmed pattern: re-cycles after a tightly-focused first pass yield substantial new pinning when the original cycle was scope-bounded (cycle 24 covered the reconnect flow but had no tests for effect dep arrays, helper integration, draw geometry, sister-hook divergence audit, edge-state restoration semantics, pre-activation Escape edge case, getNodeScreenPos internals, doDraw asymmetry). 7th consecutive clean security audit. Loop trajectory: started at 1529 baseline, now 4116 — 169% growth. Phase-2 hygiene: trimmed useOverlayCanvas unused exports (ctxRef + resize) for surface tightening on a hook extracted just 2 cycles ago.
